methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $38M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Defense & Security (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 19 same-sector peers exist in Defense & Security, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

data.narrative:
Swan is a Seed-stage defense and security company founded by Dima Kislovskiy and Narek Melik Barkhudarov that has raised $38.5M to date. The Defense & Security sector comprises 2 public comparables (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private peers including Anduril Industries, Helsing, Shield AI, Saronic, and Quantum-Systems.

The valuation analysis relies on a Stage-Progression methodology, as the Peer-Median approach is not applicable. The Defense & Security cohort contains 19 same-sector companies, but insufficient narrow-filter matches (zero same-stage, one adjacent-stage, zero similar-tier peers) prevent reliable peer triangulation. Using the $38.5M total raised as a proxy base value and applying a typical Seed-to-next-stage step-up multiplier of 2.5x yields an implied valuation of $96M.

The analysis supports a range of $82M to $111M, with a median of $96M. This range reflects ±15% sensitivity around the Stage-Progression result. The primary limitation is methodological dependence on a single approach; peer comparability data remains sparse for early-stage defense tech at this scale. Key uncertainties include the company's revenue traction, go-to-market progress, and customer concentration—none of which are reflected in this capital-based valuation framework.
